text:
There was a wise old fox who lived deep within the forest. Every morning, he sat by his favorite bush by the pond and watched the sunrise. One day, a curious rabbit hopping along noticed the fox. Intrigued by the silent observer, the rabbit hopped closer.

The fox looked up pensively for a moment, his tail swishing gently. "Come sit beside me," he said. The rabbit approached curiously.

The fox began telling the rabbit stories from his days exploring the forest. "First, there was the Great Woodrat Chase," he started. "I chased after that woodrat for hours. It turned out it was just collecting berries. Just like you!"

The rabbit listened intently, enjoying the tales. After several stories, the rabbit asked, "Can I hear more about... um... me?"

With a grin, the fox replied, "Oh, about you? Once I saw your tiny paw paw print on a fallen log. I thought it could run.
